title: Things I Forgot to Mention (Part Three)

sentences.en: Tuesday. Apparently this diary needed a whole trilogy of catch-up entries, so here is the third and, I promise, probably final one.
sentences.zh: 星期二。看來這本日記需要一整套三部曲的補寫日記，所以這是第三篇，我保證，大概是最後一篇了。

sentences.en: I never mentioned Sophia's small collection of dried beans and grain, kept in glass jars, each one labeled in handwriting older than me.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過 Sophia 收藏的一小罐一小罐乾豆和穀物，裝在玻璃罐裡，每一罐上的手寫標籤都比我年紀還大。

sentences.en: There was an afternoon I forgot to describe: William got a haircut so short he looked like a completely different, slightly startled person.
sentences.zh: 有個下午我忘了描述：William 剪了一個超短的頭髮，看起來像變了一個人，還有點被自己嚇到的樣子。

sentences.en: I never wrote about the time our art teacher praised my painting, calling the balance of colors surprisingly effective for a beginner.
sentences.zh: 我從沒寫過那次美術老師稱讚我的畫，說我對顏色的平衡處理，以一個初學者來說出乎意料地有效果。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that William once tried to aim an arrow at a paper target in PE class and hit absolutely nothing, including the target.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，William 有一次在體育課上想瞄準射一支箭射紙靶，結果什麼都沒射中，包括那個靶。

sentences.en: There was a day I never described: a sudden power blackout left half our street in darkness while the other half stayed lit, which William found deeply confusing.
sentences.zh: 有一天我從沒描述過：突然一場停電讓我們街的一半陷入黑暗，另一半卻還亮著，William 覺得這件事非常令人困惑。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention Perry's brief, dramatic period of anger toward the mail carrier, a grudge that seemed to have no clear cause.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提 Perry 曾經有一段對郵差充滿憤怒的誇張時期，這股怨氣似乎沒有任何明確的原因。

sentences.en: I never wrote about visiting a small college near our city during a family friend's graduation, watching Bernice stare at the campus like she was already imagining her future there.
sentences.zh: 我從沒寫過我們去參觀我們城市附近一間小型大學，那次是去參加一位家族朋友的畢業典禮，Bernice 盯著校園的樣子，好像已經在想像自己未來在那裡的樣子。

sentences.en: There was a moment I forgot to record: William asked Dad why some countries drive on the opposite side of the road, and Dad admitted he genuinely did not know.
sentences.zh: 有個時刻我忘了記下來：William 問爸爸為什麼有些國家開車靠對向車道，爸爸坦承他真的不知道答案。

sentences.en: I never mentioned the delicious smell of fresh bread from the bakery near school, strong enough to distract the entire class during morning reading time.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過學校附近麵包店傳出的美味麵包香，濃到足以讓全班在早自習閱讀時間分心。

sentences.en: I forgot to describe the afternoon William got genuinely excited about a new video game, an excitement so loud Perry hid under the sofa for an hour.
sentences.zh: 我忘了描述那個下午，William 對一款新電動遊戲興奮到不行，那股興奮吵到 Perry 躲到沙發底下躲了一個小時。

sentences.en: There was a small, ordinary moment I never wrote down: Mom humming quietly while ironing, completely unaware anyone was watching her from the doorway.
sentences.zh: 有個小小的平凡時刻我從沒寫下來：媽媽一邊燙衣服一邊輕輕哼歌，完全沒發現有人正從門口看著她。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our school held a short assembly about basic first aid, teaching us what to do while waiting for actual help to arrive.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，學校辦過一場關於基本急救的簡短集會，教我們在真正的救援抵達之前該怎麼做。

sentences.en: Lesson of this third catch-up entry: apparently an entire year cannot be fully captured even after three separate attempts to catch up on everything I missed.
sentences.zh: 第三篇補寫日記的心得：顯然就算試著補寫三次，也還是沒辦法完整捕捉一整年的一切。

quiz.question: What does the art teacher praise about Coco's painting?
options: The balance of colors, surprisingly effective for a beginner.

quiz.question: What does William fail to hit during PE class archery?
options: He hits it perfectly on his first try.

quiz.question: What does the school assembly teach students?
